codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature description. The blocking issue from prior synthesis (retrieve() returning empty chunk placeholders) has been fixed in commit 73b8b3f. The retrieve() method now properly: (1) stores chunks in self.chunks HashMap via index_chunk(), (2) uses inverted_index to find matching chunk_ids, (3) looks up actual chunk data with self.chunks.get(&chunk_id), (4) returns EvidenceResult with actual chunk containing non-empty text and provenance. 11 unit tests pass covering chunking, deduplication, retrieval, and reranking.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used the 'Rust' skill and followed procedure correctly. Handoff indicates skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true with no deviations.
No external services or commands were started that need to be documented in services.yaml. The feature is a pure Rust module.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Only cargo build and cargo test commands were run

sharedStateObservations.observation: Implementation follows AGENTS.md conventions: uses serde for serialization, chrono for datetime, uuid for IDs, and standard collections. No convention violations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Cargo.toml shows relevant dependencies: serde with derive/features, chrono, uuid

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that chunks needed to be stored in a HashMap for later retrieval (not just indexed in inverted_index). This was addressed in the fix commit 73b8b3f. No further knowledge gaps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 73b8b3f added chunks storage and lookup in retrieve() method

summary: Feature implementation passes scrutiny. The retrieve() method fix (73b8b3f) correctly resolves the prior blocking issue: chunks are stored in self.chunks HashMap during indexing and properly looked up during retrieval. The EvidenceResult now contains actual chunk data with non-empty text and provenance. No new blocking issues found. 11 unit tests pass.
